source: EXP-078 - corrected find strings for WIN-067 and WIN-016

patches.find: The formula that works is wholly WGS84; the formula that derives from dome geometry fails by 170×.
patches.replace:
The formula that works is wholly WGS84; the formula that derives from dome geometry fails by 170×.

The refraction cascade (Section 1.8) adds a further constraint: at the Antarctic rim (r ≈ 17,000–20,015 km), the firmament ceiling H(r) ≈ 1,400–800 km. The n(r) values implied at these radii would produce gravitational-index coupling effects catastrophically inconsistent with GRACE satellite gravimetry.
patches.rationale: EXP-078 citation addition: Antarctic rim gravity connects to cascade via n(r) at large radii.

patches.find: The dome's own AI context page lists the 'EW scale 0.9941 physical interpretation' as an open question, conceding that derived parameters lack theoretical basis.
patches.replace: The dome's own AI context page lists the 'EW scale 0.9941 physical interpretation' as an open question, conceding that derived parameters lack theoretical basis. Supplementary cascade note (Section 1.8): the n(r) refractive index values required to bend the physical sun's light by 6–29° to its apparent position would also displace all stellar positions by multiple degrees — incompatible with Gaia DR3 microarcsecond astrometry by eight orders of magnitude.
patches.rationale: EXP-078 citation addition: n(r) incompatibility with stellar astrometry is supplementary to the main Gaia refutation.
